column3:
Instructions, including exceptions to Matt Floray’s instructions applicable only to this template:
1.  Replace peripheral data in the blue boxes above with correct data for your chart, and then run the <ctrl>d and optional <ctrl>w macros.
2.  Enter all data in the charts. See Matt Floray’s instructions for data input rules. Do not manually format any cells with colors. Because Excel limits
     conditional formatting to three conditions, only some of the cells will be color fomatted when entered. Remaining cells will be correctly colored when
     Step 3 is completed. The following special procedures and exceptions to Matt’s instructions should be followed:
     a. To enter a B (breakaway), press <ctrl>b. After running the <ctrl>b macro, manually edit the cell to add an asterisk if the entry should be B*.
     b. To enter a TD not in parentheses or square brackets, press <ctrl>t.
     c. To enter a QT, press <ctrl>q. After running the <ctrl>q macro, manually edit the cell to add an asterisk if the entry should be QT*.
     d. To enter a zero in parentheses (red box) in the defensive chart, press <ctrl>n.
     e. To enter a zero in square brackets (red box) in the defensive chart, press <ctrl>g.
     f.  To enter a positive number in parentheses, enter the following keystroke where # is the 1 or 2 digit number - A#. [A - the number(s) - a period - and
         then enter]. The entry will be converted into (#) formatted as text rather than a negative number when running the macros in Step 3.
     g. To enter a simple negative yardage entry (e.g., not a negative entry after an F, INT, BK, etc., but rather, -X or -DS), enter the negative sign following
         the entry, with no space. For example, for -DS, enter as DS-. This will prevent Excel from returning an error message (expecting that the DS is a range
         name reference). The entry will be corrected when the macros in Step 3 are run.
     h. The # column following the special teams Field Goal column contains formulas that will turn cells red if the Field Goal column indicates an extra point
         attempt is no good. For teams with extra point designations that are independent of the FG column, replace the formulas in the special teams # column
         with numbers (10-39). For cells that are supposed to be colored red, enter a negative number. The cell format prints negative numbers to
         look like positive numbers, but with red cells. For any older charts thathave PAT “No Good” dice rolls printed below the offensive charts, ignore
         the “Penalty” dice rolls, as these refer to obsolete rules.
3. Only after completing all data input, press <ctrl>l. This will correct all (#) and simple negative variable yardage entries, as well as correct the color
    coding of all boxes that have not been formatted during data entry. Do not run this macro more than once!
4. Fill out the “team notes” section on the offensive chart as desired, including the spill-over / schedule section under the defense/special charts.
5. Only after proof-reading the charts, press <ctrl>z to run quantitative analysis of the offensive chart. Do not run this macro more than once.
6. Optional: if you want to replace all penalties (other than PI) with appropriate vairable yardage / modern rule penalties, press <ctrl>u. This will change all OFF and DEF
    penalties to OFF S and DEF S, with the following exceptions: (a) OFF 5 and pre-existing OFF S penalties in kickoffs are changed to OB; and (b) all OFF
    and DEF penalties in kickoff, punt and interception returns are changed to OFF R and DEF R.
